spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates rotation × flip and the displayWidth/displayHeight swap at 90/270, but the lede claims `any of the eight EXIF-style orientations` while the controls only expose four rotations × two flip values composed inline — never the full EXIF 8-value table by name.

interactive.rationale: Two selects plus a render button drive a raw canvas, an oriented canvas, a metadata panel, and a live 3×3 matrix output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises eight EXIF orientations by name. The page delivers 4×2 = 8 combinations but never labels them as EXIF orientations 1–8, which is what the blurb suggests.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers all four rotation values and both flip states, and the matrix output handles negative scale correctly. Misses: vertical flip (only horizontal exposed via the boolean), the spec's order-of-operations question, and the encoder active-orientation lock.

references.rationale: The see-also ChromeStatus link now points to the correct WebCodecs orientation feature 5128855343595520.

design_system.rationale: Variables for chrome and pill colours, but canvas pixel fills use raw hex (intended — bitmap pixels). The support pill uses paired accent + --bg-ivory text — contrast OK.

openQuestions.title: Label the 8 EXIF orientations explicitly
openQuestions.detail: The lede promises eight EXIF orientations by name. Either rename the rotation/flip pickers to a single dropdown of `EXIF 1 — Horizontal (normal)`, `EXIF 2 — Mirror horizontal`, ... `EXIF 8 — Mirror horizontal and rotate 90 CW`, or rewrite the blurb to say `four rotations × two flips = eight combinations`. The current copy claims more than the UI delivers.

openQuestions.title: Add vertical flip to the picker
openQuestions.detail: VideoFrameInit's `flip` is treated as boolean in the page UI but in Chrome's WebCodecs the equivalent is the union `"none"|"horizontal"|"vertical"`. The sibling video-orient demo exposes all three; this one drops vertical for no reason.

openQuestions.title: Compute matrix from VideoFrame when available
openQuestions.detail: Rather than computing the matrix from the rot/flip controls, the page should construct a VideoFrame with those settings, read frame.rotation and frame.flip back, and derive the matrix from those — that way the readout reflects what the engine actually stored, not what the controls said.

summary: Useful matrix-and-dimensions teaching panel with the corrected ChromeStatus reference. The remaining issue is that the lede still oversells `eight EXIF orientations` when the UI exposes the 4×2 rotation/flip cross product.
